label: Glucosamine
title: Natural Compound: A natural Sugar found in Shellfish Shells, Fungi, and the human body; a building block of Cartilage that supports joint health. (22)

label: Collagen (Type I, V, X)
title: Natural Compound: Collagen Type I is the primary form in the human body, supporting Skin, Hair, Nails, and Bone Health. Type V supports the formation of Cornea Tissues, certain Skin layers, Hair, and the Placenta. Type X is a short-chain, non-fibrillar Collagen found in calcified Cartilage, important for skeletal growth and used as a biomarker for Osteoarthritis. (48, 150)

label: Chondroitin
title: Natural Compound: A substance in Cartilage that helps maintain joint structure and cushioning. (22)

label: Gelatin
title: Natural Compound: A Protein from boiled Animal Tissues, used as a Gelling Agent, Thickener, or Stabilizer in Foods, Supplements, and industrial products. ()

label: Collagen (Type I, III)
title: Natural Compound: Collagen Type I makes up about 90% of the human body Collagen, supporting the structure of Skin, Tendons, Ligaments, and Bones, while Type III supports Muscles, Arteries, and Organs. These 2 types of Collagen are commonly combined in Supplements for aging. (150)

label: Marine Collagen (Type I)
title: Natural Compound: Collagen from Fish or other marine life, with smaller peptides thought to be more easily absorbed by the body. (150)

label: MSM (Methylsulfonylmethane)
title: Natural Compound: A natural organic Sulfur Compound found in many organisms, often used as a Supplement for joint and skin health. ()

label: Hyaluronic Acid
title: Natural Compound: A Polysaccharide in Connective Tissues and Skin that retains moisture, supporting hydration, joint lubrication, and tissue repair. ()

label: Surimi
title: Processed Food Product: Paste made from minced, rinsed White Fish—commonly Alaska Pollock or Pacific Whiting—used to make Seafood Products like Crab Sticks. ()

label: Collagen (Type II)
title: Natural Compound: Collagen Type II provides joint support, found in elastic Cartilage of human and animals. (150)

label: Excessive Fertilisation
title: Overuse of chemical fertilisers that can harm soil and water quality.. Often relates to Agriculture

label: Industrial Waste
title: Production of hazardous or non-recyclable waste from industrial activities.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Plastic Pollution
title: Accumulation of plastic waste in the environment, affecting wildlife and ecosystems.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Energy Overconsumption
title: High use of fossil fuels or non-renewable energy sources.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Overfishing
title: Harvesting marine species beyond sustainable limits.. Often relates to Fishing

label: Mining Activities
title: Extraction of minerals, including rare earths, often impacting ecosystems.. Often relates to Energy Production

label: Urbanisation
title: Expansion of urban areas into natural landscapes.. Often relates to Urban Development

label: Labour Exploitation
title: Unfair labor practices, including child labor and unsafe working conditions.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: IUU Fishing
title: Illegal, unreported, or unregulated fishing practices.. Often relates to Fishing

label: Soil Fertility Decline
title: Loss of essential nutrients and soil quality over time.. Often relates to Agriculture

label: Pesticide & Herbicide Use
title: Application of synthetic chemicals to control pests and weeds.. Often relates to Agriculture

label: Water Overuse
title: Excessive withdrawal of groundwater or surface water beyond sustainable levels.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Soil Degradation
title: Loss of soil nutrients or structure due to unsustainable farming practices.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Deforestation
title: Clearing forests for agriculture, development, or other human activities.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Monoculture Farming
title: Growing a single crop species over large areas, reducing biodiversity and soil health.. Often relates to Agriculture

label: Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions
title: Release of carbon dioxide, methane, and other greenhouse gases from human activities.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Antibiotic Overuse
title: Excessive use of antibiotics in agriculture or medicine, contributing to resistance.. Often relates to Various Industries

label: Genetic Modification
title: Use of genetically modified organisms in agriculture.. Often relates to Agriculture

label: Water Contamination
title: Degradation of water quality due to pollutants.

label: Chemical Exposure
title: Contact of humans or wildlife with toxic substances.

label: Antibiotic Resistance
title: Increased resistance of bacteria to antibiotics.

label: Resource Scarcity
title: Depletion of natural resources such as water or minerals.

label: Resistant Pests & Weeds
title: Development of resistance in pests or weeds to chemical controls.

label: Loss of Natural Ecosystems
title: Disappearance or severe degradation of natural environments.

label: Eutrophication
title: Excess nutrients in water causing algal blooms and aquatic dead zones.

label: Marine Biodiversity Loss
title: Decline in populations of aquatic species.

label: Soil Contamination
title: Accumulation of harmful substances in soil.

label: Air Pollution
title: Release of particulates or gases into the atmosphere.

label: Biodiversity Loss
title: Reduction in species diversity or population.

label: Ocean Acidification
title: Decrease in ocean pH caused by CO₂ absorption.

label: Climate Change
title: Global warming and altered weather patterns.

label: Groundwater Depletion
title: Reduction of underground water reserves.

label: Soil Erosion
title: Loss of topsoil due to wind or water.

label: Habitat Fragmentation / Habitat Loss
title: Division or destruction of habitats into smaller, isolated patches.

label: Unsafe Drinking Water
title: Contaminated water that is unfit for human consumption.

label: Reduced Agricultural Yield
title: Decreased productivity of crops or livestock.

label: Sea Level Rise
title: Rising ocean levels that increase coastal flooding and erosion.

label: Natural Disaster Increase
title: Higher frequency or intensity of extreme weather events.

label: Social Inequality
title: Unequal access to resources, services, or opportunities.

label: Harmful Algal Blooms (HABs)
title: Rapid growth of toxin-producing algae in water bodies, posing risks to ecosystems and human health.

label: Dead Zones (Hypoxia)
title: Areas of water with very low oxygen levels, unable to support most aquatic life.

label: Algal Blooms
title: Excessive growth of algae, usually driven by nutrient pollution.

label: Aquatic Life Loss
title: Decline in aquatic species abundance and biodiversity.

label: Ecosystem Degradation/Collapse
title: Breakdown of ecosystem structure, function, and resilience.

label: Species Extinction
title: Permanent loss of a species from the planet.

label: Human Health Impacts
title: Widespread health problems linked to environmental conditions.

label: Food Insecurity
title: Lack of reliable access to sufficient, safe, and nutritious food.

label: Economic Decline
title: Sustained reduction in economic activity and productivity.

label: Resource Conflicts
title: Disputes or tensions over scarce natural resources.

label: Corn Farming
title: Cultivation of Corn (Maize), a major global agricultural sector with high production volumes.

label: Cattle Farming
title: Rearing Cattle for Beef or Dairy, including breeding, feeding, and herd management.

label: Cattle Slaughtering
title: Processing of live Cattle into carcasses and raw products for further use in food and byproducts.

label: Dairy Farming
title: Milking cattle and initial processing of raw Milk for consumption or further Dairy production.

label: Tanning Industry
title: Treating Animal Skins and Hides to produce Leather products.

label: Fisheries
title: Harvesting Fish and Aquatic Species from wild marine and freshwater stocks, including coastal, inland, and deep-sea ecosystems.

label: Shark Fisheries
title: Targeted capture of Sharks for Fins, Meat, Cartilage, and other derived products.

label: Pig Farming
title: Breeding and raising Pigs for Meat, including commercial and small-scale operations.

label: Pig Slaughtering
title: Processing of live Pigs into carcasses and raw products for further food or byproduct use.

label: Livestock Trading (Bovine)
title: Import and export of live Cattle for breeding, fattening, or slaughter.

label: Livestock Trading (Porcine)
title: Import and export of live pigs for breeding or slaughter.

label: Rendering (Animals)
title: Collection and processing of animal byproducts—Bones, Cartilage, Hides, Fat, Offal, and Blood—into usable materials.

label: Pharmaceuticals Industry
title: Producing Drugs, Vaccines, and Biologics for human and animal health.

label: Pet Food Industry
title: Manufacturing nutritional products formulated for domestic animals.

label: Cosmetics Industry
title: Creating personal care and beauty products for external human use.

label: Animal Feed Production
title: Producing feed to support the health and productivity of Livestock and Poultry.

label: Fertiliser Production
title: Converting organic or synthetic materials into nutrients that enhance Soil Fertility and Crop Growth.

label: Poultry Farming (Broilers)
title: Breeding and raising Birds—primarily Chickens, Turkeys, and Ducks—for Meat.

label: Livestock Trading (Poultry)
title: Import and export of live Poultry, including Chicks, breeding stock, or slaughter-ready Birds, for breeding, fattening, or processing.

label: Crustacean Processing
title: Handling and preparation of Crustaceans—commonly Shrimp, Crabs, Lobsters, and Crayfish—for consumption.

label: Poultry Slaughtering
title: Processing live Birds into carcasses, cuts, and byproducts such as Feathers and Offal.

label: Paper Manufacturing
title: Transforming Wood or recycled Fibers into usable Paper products.

label: Forestry Industry
title: Managing, cultivating, and conserving forests and their resources.

label: Fish Aquaculture
title: Farming, breeding, and raising Fish in controlled environments.

label: Commercial Fishing
title: Catching Fish and Seafood from wild marine and freshwater stocks for profit.

label: Artisanal Fishing
title: Small-scale, low-tech fishing by individuals or households, mainly for local consumption or trade.

label: Layer Poultry Farming/Egg Production
title: Breeding and raising Female Chickens (Hens) for Commercial Egg Production.

label: Egg Breaking Industry
title: Egg Breaking processes Eggs into cracked, separated, frozen, liquid, dried, and pasteurised forms for use in Bakeries, Pasta Production, and wider Food Manufacturing.

label: Crustacean Fisheries
title: Commercial harvesting of aquatic arthropods, mainly decapods like Shrimp, Lobsters, and Crabs.

label: Biotechnology
title: Use of biological systems and organisms to develop new products, processes, and technologies.

label: Shrimp and Prawn Farming
title: Aquaculture of heavily farmed Crustaceans, including Whiteleg Shrimp, Giant Tiger Prawn, and Freshwater Prawn.

label: Lobster Farming
title: Farming of Rock Lobsters; a small share of total lobster production compared with wild catch (<5%).

label: Crab Farming
title: Growing aquaculture sector, though wild-caught fisheries still dominate (1.6 million tons wild vs 1.24 million tons farmed).

label: Crayfish Farming
title: Smaller-scale aquaculture sector with production around 200,000 metric tons, less than Shrimp, Prawn, or Crab.

label: Joint Health Supplements
title: Tablets, capsules, gummies, or powders containing Glucosamine, often combined with Chondroitin Sulfate or other compounds, used to support joint function, reduce joint pain, and help slow cartilage degradation—commonly taken for osteoarthritis and joint mobility support.

label: Confectionery (general)
title: Foods high in sugar and carbohydrates.

label: Capsules
title: Cylindrical shells, often gelatin or plant-based, used to deliver medications or supplements, designed for easy swallowing and controlled release.

label: Collagen Peptides/Hydrolysed Collagen
title: Small fragments of animal collagen broken into absorbable peptides or amino acids, available in powders, pills, or liquids.

label: Collagen Face Cream
title: Skincare product with hydrolysed collagen to support skin elasticity, hydration, and a smoother appearance.

label: Sausages
title: Processed meat made from ground or emulsified meat, fat, and seasonings, encased in natural or synthetic casings.

label: Coated Tablets
title: Compressed tablets covered with a polymer, sugar, or film to improve stability, mask taste, or control release.

label: Wound Dressings
title: Sterile materials applied to wounds to protect, absorb fluids, control moisture, and support healing.

label: Pet Supplements
title: Nutritional products for companion animals, containing vitamins, minerals, amino acids, or other beneficial compounds.

label: Energy Gels
title: Concentrated, easy-to-digest carbohydrate products with electrolytes, designed for rapid energy during endurance activities.

label: Protein Bars
title: Compact foods with protein, carbohydrates, fats, and nutrients for convenient supplementation.

label: Gummies
title: Soft, chewy, fruit-flavored sweets, typically molded into various shapes.

label: Photographic Film
title: Plastic strips coated with light-sensitive chemicals for analog photography.

label: Crab Sticks
title: Processed seafood made from minced white fish, shaped and flavored to mimic crab meat.

label: Jello
title: A wiggly, fruit-flavored gelatin dessert, typically served chilled.

label: Marshmallows
title: Fluffy, sweet confections commonly used in desserts or hot drinks.

label: Dairy Yogurt
title: A creamy, fermented dairy product made from milk.

label: Cream Cheese
title: A soft, spreadable fresh cheese.

label: Sour Cream
title: A tangy, cultured dairy topping.

label: Margarine
title: A vegetable oil–based spread commonly used as a butter substitute.

label: Ice Cream
title: A frozen, sweet dairy dessert.

label: Crustacean Shells
title: Exoskeletons from Shrimp, Lobsters, Crabs, and Crayfish, commonly used to produce Chitin, Chitosan, and Glucosamine.

label: Wild-Caught Fish
title: Fish harvested from natural marine and freshwater environments for Food, Supplements, and byproducts, including Cod, Tuna, Anchovies, and Sardines.

label: Eggs
title: Mass produced Eggs (predominantly from Chicken), typically laid by selectively bred Laying Hens in controlled, automated, and specialised farming systems.

label: Layer Poultry
title: Poultry (mostly Chickens) bred and raised for Eggs, commonly called Layers or Laying Hens.

label: Filamentuous Fungi
title: Saprophytic microorganisms used in industries to produce enzymes, chemicals, and other compounds.

label: Bovine (Cow)
title: Domesticated cattle raised for Beef, Dairy, Leather, and byproducts such as Gelatin, Tallow, and Bone Meal.

label: Sharks and Elasmobranchs
title: Cartilaginous fish harvested for Meat, Fins, Cartilage, Liver Oil, and Skin; byproducts are used in Supplements, Cosmetics, and traditional medicine.

label: Porcine (Pig)
title: Domesticated pigs raised for Meat (Pork) and byproducts like Insulin, Gelatin, Heparin, and Lard.

label: Broiler Avian (Poultry/Fowls)
title: Birds such as Chickens and Ducks, raised primarily for Meat.

label: Farmed Fish
title: Fish raised in aquaculture systems for Food, nutritional Supplements, and other byproducts, commonly including Salmon, Tilapia, Catfish, and Carp.

label: Dairy Cows
title: Cattle bred for high Milk production, central to the Dairy Industry for Milk, Cheese, Yogurt, and other Dairy Products.

label: Lignin (Wood)
title: A complex polymer in woody plant cell walls that provides rigidity and decay resistance, often obtained as a byproduct of the Pulp and Paper Industry.

label: Chitin
title: A natural polymer derived from the exoskeletons of arthropods and the cell walls of fungi.

label: Bovine Hide
title: Thick, collagen-rich Skins from Cattle. A major raw material for Leather production and an important source for Gelatin, Collagen, and medical-grade biomaterials.

label: Bovine Connective Tissues (Tendons, Ligaments, and Fascia)
title: Strong connective tissues from cattle, including tendons, ligaments, and fascia, rich in Type I Collagen. Widely used for Joint Health Supplements, Sports Nutrition, and medical or surgical materials.

label: Fish Scales & Skin
title: Collagen-rich outer tissues of fish, processed for Gelatin, Collagen peptides, Cosmetics, and Biomedical or packaging applications like Wound Dressings and Biofilms.

label: Fish Byproducts (heads, bones, fins, vertebrae, cartilage)
title: Various Fish Byproducts—such as heads, bones, and fins—processed for Collagen extraction, Fish Protein Hydrolysates, Animal Feed, Fertilizers, and Biomedical uses

label: DMS (Dimethyl Sulfide)
title: A sulfur-containing compound formed from the breakdown of Lignin in Wood Pulp during Paper Manufacturing.

label: DMSO (Dimethyl Sulfoxide)
title: A colorless, odorless organic solvent derived from oxidizing Dimethyl Sulfide (DMS), used in industrial applications and as a precursor for Supplements like MSM.

label: Rooster Combs
title: The fleshy, red crest on a rooster’s head, more pronounced than in hens.

label: Bacteria (Streptococcus spp. or Bacillus spp.)
title: Microorganisms used in fermentation to produce Hyaluronic Acid, as well as in Food Processing, Enzyme production, Probiotics, and other industrial applications.

label: Fish Meat & Products
title: Fish flesh and related products, primarily consumed as food.

label: Porcine Bones
title: Skeletal material from Pigs with slightly lower density and a distinct fat profile compared to Bovine Bones. Commonly used for Gelatin, Bone Broth, and food-grade mineral extraction.

label: Corn
title: Also called maize; widely grown for food, animal feed, and various industrial uses.

label: Avian (Poultry) Bones
title: Lightweight, porous Bones from Poultry such as Chickens, Ducks, and Turkeys. Primarily used in Animal Feed Supplements, Hydroxyapatite extraction, and niche Gelatin or Broth products.

label: Avian Skin
title: Thin skins from Poultry, especially Chickens. Most commonly rendered for Animal Fats, but also used in smaller volumes for Broths and Collagen Peptide production.

label: Pig Skin
title: Softer skin with higher fat content than Bovine Hides. Widely processed into food-grade Gelatin, Sausage Casings, cosmetic-grade Collagen, and traditional pork products.

label: Porcine Connective Tissues (Tendons, Ligaments, and Fascia)
title: Flexible connective tissues from Pigs, processed similarly to Bovine sources. Used for Collagen, Gelatin, and nutraceutical ingredients, with slightly different yield and processing characteristics.

label: Poultry Connective Tissues (Tendons, Ligaments, and Fascia)
title: Smaller connective tissues from Poultry. Less commonly used at industrial scale, but still processed into Broths and low-yield Collagen sources.

label: Eggshell Membrane
title: Thin, resilient layer between an Avian Egg's Hard Shell and the Egg White, composed of Collagen (Type I, V, and X), Glucosamine, Chondroitin Sulphate, Hyaluronic Acid, Elastin, Keratin, and many other Proteins. Historically discarded as waste but now prized as a highly sustainable and bioactive source for Health and Beauty Products

label: Animal Cartilage
title: Tough Connective Tissue from Cattle, Poultry, or Fish, used to extract Collagen, Gelatin, and Chondroitin for supplements, foods, pet feed, and biomedical applications.

label: Shark Cartilage
title: High-value cartilage from shark skeletons (byproducts of meat and fin industries), mainly used in nutraceuticals and cosmetics.

label: Beef (Bovine Meat)
title: Meat from cattle, processed for human consumption and used in a variety of food products.

label: Pork (Pig Meat)
title: Meat from pigs, processed for human consumption and widely used in global food products.

label: Poultry (Avian Meat)
title: Meat from birds such as chicken, turkey, and duck, processed for human consumption and used worldwide.

label: Bovine Bones
title: Dense skeletal structures from Cattle, rich in Type I Collagen and minerals such as Calcium Phosphate. Widely processed for Gelatin, bone-derived ingredients, Pet Food, Calcium Supplements, and biomedical applications.
